Benchmark/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Benchmark | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Benchmark/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Benchmark The Benchmark folder contains the headless benchmarking subsystem. It can run multiple routing strategies against the same scenario and produce structured, JSON-serialisable results for comparison. Files File Responsibility BenchmarkConfig.cs Fully serialisable scenario definition: initial device list, scheduled events, engine settings, and the list of router names to compare. BenchmarkRunner.cs Headless tick loop. For each router in BenchmarkConfig.RouterNames it resets the engine, applies the config, fires scheduled events at their ticks, runs the simulation for DurationTicks, and captures a BenchmarkResult. Returns a BenchmarkSession. BenchmarkResult.cs Per-router output: final metric values and the full TickSnapshot[] history for charting. BenchmarkSession.cs Root JSON document combining BenchmarkConfig with all BenchmarkResult entries. Self-contained for saving and sharing. BenchmarkEventEntry.cs Tick-stamped event union. Supported types: ToggleBenchmarkEvent (Hub sends a ControlPacket), RemoveDeviceBenchmarkEvent (simulates node failure), AddDeviceBenchmarkEvent (simulates node join). Uses System.Text.Json polymorphic $type discriminators. DeviceBenchmarkDto.cs Serialisable record describing a device (type, name, position, frequency). Used inside BenchmarkConfig and AddDeviceBenchmarkEvent. Supports with-expression copying. Benchmark flow BenchmarkConfig +- BenchmarkRunner.Run() for each router: 1. Engine.Reset() + Statistics.Reset() 2. Apply settings + register initial devices 3. Tick loop [0 .. DurationTicks]: - fire scheduled BenchmarkEventEntry at matching ticks - Engine.Tick() 4. Capture BenchmarkResult (metrics + TickSnapshot[]) +- Returns BenchmarkSession { Config, Results[] } Extension points Add new BenchmarkEventEntry subtypes to schedule additional in-simulation actions (e.g. topology rebuild, router swap mid-run). Parent See Engine/Documentation.md for the full engine architecture.

Core/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Core | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Core/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Core The Core folder contains the central runtime of the simulation: the singleton engine, its strongly-typed event definitions, and the exception thrown when the in-flight packet limit is exceeded. Files File Responsibility SimulationEngine.cs Singleton tick-loop driver. Owns the device registry, packet priority queue, pluggable Router and NetworkBuilder, and Topology. Each Tick() call increments the tick counter, fires TickEvent, dispatches due packets, and raises Ticked. SimulationEvents.cs All strongly-typed EventArgs used by SimulationEngine events: PacketRegisteredEventArgs, PacketDeliveredEventArgs, PacketExpiredEventArgs, DeviceRegisteredEventArgs, DeviceRemovedEventArgs, TickedEventArgs. PacketLimitExceededException.cs Thrown by SimulationEngine.RegisterPacket when the in-flight packet count reaches or exceeds EffectivePacketLimit. Carries Limit, ActualCount, and AtTick. Key behaviors Singleton pattern - SimulationEngine.Instance and SimulationStatistics.Instance are process-level singletons; WASM runs a single instance shared by the whole browser session. Packet limit guard - prevents unbounded packet storms in flooding topologies. Auto-scales with device count unless MaxActivePackets is set manually. Pluggable strategies - Router (type IPacketRouter) and NetworkBuilder (type INetworkBuilder) can be replaced at runtime without restarting the simulation. Parent See Engine/Documentation.md for the full engine architecture.

Devices/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Devices | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Devices/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Devices The Devices folder contains the abstract base class and all concrete device types that participate in the simulation. Files File Responsibility Device.cs Abstract base for all devices. Holds a unique Id (GUID), a mutable Name, and a 2D Position (Vector2). Defines Recieve(Packet) which stamps PreviousHop, sends a ConfirmationPacket if requested, and delegates re-routing to SimulationEngine.RoutePacket. HubDevice.cs Central gateway device; the intended destination for all packets from GeneratorDevice. Overrides Recieve to also send queued ControlPacket commands to EmitterDevice instances on each tick at ControlFrequencyTicks (delegated via SimulationEngine.TickEvent). GeneratorDevice.cs Sensor-like device that self-emits one data packet toward the Hub every GenFrequencyTicks ticks. Subscribes to SimulationEngine.TickEvent on construction. EmitterDevice.cs Actuator-like device that accepts ControlPacket commands from the Hub. Maintains a boolean State (on/off). The Hub drives it by sending ControlPacket at ControlFrequencyTicks intervals. Inheritance Device (abstract) +-- HubDevice +-- GeneratorDevice +-- EmitterDevice Extension points Add new device types by subclassing Device and optionally subscribing to SimulationEngine.TickEvent for periodic behavior. Parent See Engine/Documentation.md for full engine architecture.

Documentation.html.title: Engine - IoT Swarm Mesh Simulation Engine | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Documentation.html.summary:
Engine - IoT Swarm Mesh Simulation Engine The Engine library is the self-contained simulation core of the IoT Swarm Mesh project. It models a wireless IoT network on a 2D plane: devices are placed at arbitrary positions, data packets flow between them tick-by-tick, and a pluggable routing algorithm propagates data toward the central hub. The library has no UI dependencies and can be driven by any host (console, Blazor WebAssembly, test harness) that calls SimulationEngine.Instance.Tick(). Architecture overview graph TD SE["SimulationEngine (singleton)
tick loop * device registry
packet priority queue * typed events
Router: IPacketRouter
NetworkBuilder: INetworkBuilder
Topology: INetworkTopology"] subgraph "Routing abstractions (Engine/Routers/)" IR["IPacketRouter
<<interface>>

<<interface>>
GetVisibleDevices * GetConnectedDevices
AreVisible * AreConnected"] IMT["IMutableNetworkTopology

RemoveDevice * ClearConnections"] IB["INetworkBuilder
<<interface>>

(WebApp default)"] NT["NetworkTopology
implements IMutableNetworkTopology
adjacency set + on-the-fly visibility"] FMB["FullMeshNetworkBuilder
implements INetworkBuilder
connects every visible pair"] end subgraph Devices D["Device (abstract)
identity * position
Receive -> SE.RoutePacket"] Hub["HubDevice"] Gen["GeneratorDevice
SE.RoutePacket on tick"] Emit["EmitterDevice"] end subgraph Packets P["Packet"] PD["PacketData"] CP["ConfirmationPacket"] end subgraph Events["Core/SimulationEvents.cs"] EV["PacketRegistered * PacketDelivered
PacketExpired * DeviceRegistered
DeviceRemoved * Ticked"] end SE -- "owns" --> IR SE -- "owns" --> IB SE -- "owns" --> IMT SE -- raises --> EV IR -.implements.-> FPR IR -.implements.-> SFR IB -.implements.-> FMB IMT -.implements.-> NT IT -.extends.-> IMT SE -->|TickEvent| Gen SE -->|RoutePacket| IR IR -->|Route(packet, sender, topology)| IT IB -->|Build(devices, topology)| IMT D -->|Receive -> RoutePacket| SE Hub & Gen & Emit -->|extend| D CP -->|extends| P P -->|carries| PD Key concepts Tick-based time model SimulationEngine is the singleton clock. Each call to Tick() represents one simulation step: TickCount is incremented. Wall-clock delta time (dt) is measured. TickEvent is raised - subscribed devices perform periodic work (e.g. GeneratorDevice emitting a packet). All packets whose ArrivalTick <= TickCount are dequeued and dispatched to their NextHop device. Ticked is raised with the final tick count and dt for observers such as SimulationStatistics. Visibility vs. Connection Two distinct spatial concepts are kept separate by design: Concept What it means Used for Visibility Euclidean distance <= VisibilityDistance Broadcast (flooding), discovery, precondition for connection Connection Explicit logical link managed by INetworkBuilder Unicast routing, network-topology algorithms A device can be visible to many neighbours but connected to only a subset of them (subject to per-device connection limits, protocol decisions, etc.). INetworkTopology exposes both concepts: topology.GetVisibleDevices(device) // all devices within radio range topology.GetConnectedDevices(device) // explicitly connected neighbours topology.AreVisible(a, b) topology.AreConnected(a, b) Pluggable routing - IPacketRouter IPacketRouter is the single abstraction for all routing strategies. public interface IPacketRouter { string Name { get; } void Route(Packet packet, Device sender, INetworkTopology topology); } The active router is stored on SimulationEngine.Router and can be replaced at runtime: SimulationEngine.Instance.Router = new MyCustomRouter(); Devices never reference a router directly - they call SimulationEngine.Instance.RoutePacket(packet, this), which delegates to the currently configured IPacketRouter. Bundled implementations Class Strategy FloodingPacketRouter Engine default (SimulationEngine.Router). Naive broadcast - clones the packet to every visible neighbour. SmartFloodingPacketRouter WebApp default (SimulationConfig.SelectedRouter). Sends directly to destination if visible; otherwise floods all visible neighbours except From and PreviousHop. Note: SimulationEngine.Router initialises to FloodingPacketRouter. The Blazor WebApp overrides this to SmartFloodingPacketRouter via SimulationConfig.SelectedRouter, which SimulationService applies on construction. Packet.PreviousHop is set by Device.Recieve just before re-routing so the smart router knows which device the packet came from and can avoid sending it back. Pluggable network formation - INetworkBuilder INetworkBuilder is the abstraction for mesh-formation algorithms. public interface INetworkBuilder { string Name { get; } void Build(IReadOnlyList<Device> devices, IMutableNetworkTopology topology); } The active builder is stored on SimulationEngine.NetworkBuilder and is called automatically whenever the device registry changes. It can also be replaced and re-applied at runtime: SimulationEngine.Instance.NetworkBuilder = new MyMeshBuilder(); SimulationEngine.Instance.RebuildTopology(); Bundled implementations Class Strategy FullMeshNetworkBuilder Connects every mutually visible pair (original implicit behaviour) Network topology - INetworkTopology / IMutableNetworkTopology NetworkTopology is the live topology object owned by SimulationEngine. Read-only view (INetworkTopology) is exposed as SimulationEngine.Topology and passed to every IPacketRouter.Route call. Mutable view (IMutableNetworkTopology) is passed only to INetworkBuilder.Build so topology mutations are centralised. Visibility is computed on-the-fly (no caching) so it always reflects current device positions. Connections are an in-memory adjacency set updated by the builder. Packet lifetime A Packet carries a source (From), destination (To), next-hop device, arrival tick, and a TTL counter. Each hop decrements TTL; a packet reaching zero is silently dropped (raises PacketExpired). If NeedConfirmation is set, the destination automatically routes a ConfirmationPacket back to the originator. Flood-clone identity fields Because flooding broadcasts cloned copies of a packet to every visible neighbour, two extra fields are stamped on Packet to make statistical analysis of these copies possible: Field Set when Preserved by Clone() Purpose OriginId Constructor - equals Id on original (yes) (MemberwiseClone) Groups all clones of one logical message InitialTtl First RegisterPacket call (when InitialTtl == 0) (yes) Enables hop-count calculation: hops = InitialTtl - TTL These fields drive the two new statistics metrics (see below). Active-packet limit SimulationEngine enforces a configurable upper bound on the number of simultaneously in-flight packets to prevent packet storms (e.g. routing loops in flooding networks) from growing unbounded. Property Default Meaning MaxActivePackets 0 0 = automatic limit EffectivePacketLimit devices x 2000 The limit actually enforced on each RegisterPacket call AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE 2000 Multiplier for the automatic calculation Automatic mode (MaxActivePackets = 0): The limit is recomputed on every RegisterPacket call as Devices.Count x AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE. This means the limit automatically scales up as more devices are added to the simulation. Manual override: Set MaxActivePackets to any positive integer to fix the limit regardless of device count. The UI settings panel exposes this value and shows the current automatic limit as a hint. Violation behaviour: When _packets.Count >= EffectivePacketLimit, RegisterPacket throws PacketLimitExceededException containing the limit, actual count, and the tick at which the violation occurred. SimulationService in the Blazor client catches this exception, stops the tick loop, and stores the message in PacketLimitError which is rendered as a red error banner. active packets limit not reached -> packet enqueued normally active packets >= limit -> PacketLimitExceededException thrown -> RunLoopAsync catches it -> IsRunning = false, PacketLimitError set -> error banner shown in UI Project structure Path Responsibility Core/SimulationEngine.cs Singleton engine: tick loop, device registry, packet queue, typed events, Router + NetworkBuilder + Topology, packet-limit guard Core/SimulationEvents.cs Strongly-typed EventArgs for all engine events Core/PacketLimitExceededException.cs Exception thrown when the in-flight packet count exceeds MaxActivePackets Devices/Device.cs Abstract base: identity, 2D position, receive / forward via SimulationEngine.RoutePacket Devices/HubDevice.cs Central gateway; destination for all GeneratorDevice packets Devices/GeneratorDevice.cs Emits one packet per GenFrequencyTicks ticks via RoutePacket Devices/EmitterDevice.cs Receives ControlPacket and toggles boolean state; Hub drives it at ControlFrequencyTicks Packets/Packet.cs Core transmission unit: routing metadata, TTL, OriginId, InitialTtl, payload Packets/PacketData.cs Untyped application-level payload wrapper Packets/ConfirmationPacket.cs Delivery acknowledgement routed back to the originator Packets/ControlPacket.cs Hub->Emitter command packet carrying a bool Command (on/off) Routers/Contracts/IPacketRouter.cs < > routing strategy contract Routers/Contracts/INetworkTopology.cs < > read-only topology: visibility + connections Routers/Contracts/IMutableNetworkTopology.cs < > topology mutations (builder-only) Routers/Contracts/INetworkBuilder.cs < > network-formation strategy contract Routers/Topology/NetworkTopology.cs Concrete topology: on-the-fly visibility + adjacency-set connections Routers/Builders/FullMeshNetworkBuilder.cs FullMeshNetworkBuilder - connect every visible pair Routers/Flooding/PacketRouter.cs FloodingPacketRouter - broadcast to all visible neighbours (engine default) Routers/SmartFlooding/SmartFloodingPacketRouter.cs SmartFloodingPacketRouter - direct if destination visible; filtered flood otherwise (WebApp default) Statistics/SimulationStatistics.cs Event-driven singleton: ten metrics + history ring-buffer Statistics/StatMetric.cs Single observable metric with display formatting and plottable flag Statistics/TickSnapshot.cs Immutable per-tick value record for time-series charting (8 plottable fields) Benchmark/BenchmarkConfig.cs Fully serialisable scenario: devices + events + settings + router list Benchmark/BenchmarkEventEntry.cs Tick-stamped event union: Toggle / RemoveDevice / AddDevice Benchmark/DeviceBenchmarkDto.cs Serialisable device description (record, supports with) Benchmark/BenchmarkResult.cs Per-router final metrics + full TickSnapshot[] history Benchmark/BenchmarkSession.cs Root JSON document: config + list of results, self-contained for sharing Benchmark/BenchmarkRunner.cs Headless tick loop: resets engine, applies events, runs N routers, returns session Comparing protocols on the same network Because both Router and NetworkBuilder are hot-swappable properties on the singleton engine, you can: Run a fixed network for N ticks with protocol A -> collect SimulationStatistics. Call SimulationEngine.Instance.Router = protocolB; SimulationEngine.Instance.Reset() -> run again. Compare the two SimulationStatistics snapshots side-by-side in the UI. The Benchmark subsystem (Engine/Benchmark/) automates this workflow end-to-end: BenchmarkConfig <- user defines: devices, events, settings, router names | v BenchmarkRunner.Run() <- headless loop; one full reset+run per router | for each router: | 1. engine.Reset() + stats.Reset() | 2. apply engine settings + register initial devices | 3. tick loop (0..DurationTicks): | - fire scheduled BenchmarkEvents at matching ticks | - engine.Tick() | 4. capture BenchmarkResult (final metrics + TickSnapshot[]) | v BenchmarkSession <- { Config, Results[] } - JSON-serialisable Scheduled events (BenchmarkEventEntry) supported: Type Effect ToggleBenchmarkEvent(deviceName) Hub sends a ControlPacket toggle to the named emitter RemoveDeviceBenchmarkEvent(deviceName) Device is removed from the engine (simulates node failure) AddDeviceBenchmarkEvent(dto) New device is registered (simulates node joining) Save / Load: BenchmarkSession serialises to compact JSON via System.Text.Json with polymorphic $type discriminators on BenchmarkEvent. The Blazor BenchmarkService exposes Serialize / Deserialize and triggers a browser file download via downloadTextFile JS interop. Documentation hierarchy Each subfolder contains its own Documentation.md: Core/Documentation.md - engine singleton, events, packet-limit exception. Devices/Documentation.md - device base class and all concrete device types. Packets/Documentation.md - packet types and payload. Routers/Documentation.md - routing subsystem overview and design principles. Routers/Contracts/Documentation.md - all routing and topology interfaces. Routers/Topology/Documentation.md - NetworkTopology implementation. Routers/Builders/Documentation.md - network-formation strategies. Routers/Flooding/Documentation.md - FloodingPacketRouter. Routers/SmartFlooding/Documentation.md - SmartFloodingPacketRouter. Statistics/Documentation.md - metrics, observable values, history ring buffer. Benchmark/Documentation.md - headless benchmark runner and session types.

Packets/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Packets | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Packets/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Packets The Packets folder contains all packet types that travel through the simulation network, along with the untyped payload wrapper. Files File Responsibility Packet.cs Core transmission unit. Carries From, To, NextHop, ArrivalTick, TTL, OriginId, InitialTtl, and a PacketData payload. Clone() creates a shallow copy preserving OriginId for flood-clone tracking by SimulationStatistics. PacketData.cs Untyped payload wrapper. The Data property holds any application-level value (sensor reading, command flag, raw bytes). The receiving device is responsible for casting to the expected type. ConfirmationPacket.cs Acknowledgement packet routed back to the original sender when Packet.NeedConfirmation is true. Swaps From and To of the original packet and carries the original Id encoded as bytes. ControlPacket.cs Command packet sent by HubDevice to a specific EmitterDevice. Carries a bool Command (true = on, false = off) that the emitter applies to its State on receipt. Key concepts TTL - decremented on every hop; packet is dropped and PacketExpired is raised when TTL reaches zero. OriginId - equals Id on the original; preserved through Clone() so statistics can detect duplicate flood-clone deliveries. InitialTtl - stamped once by SimulationEngine.RegisterPacket on first enqueue; enables hop-count calculation as InitialTtl - TTL at delivery. Parent See Engine/Documentation.md for the full engine architecture.

Routers/Builders/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Routers/Builders | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Routers/Builders/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Routers/Builders This folder contains network-formation strategy implementations (classes that implement INetworkBuilder). Each builder decides which pairs of devices become connected after a topology rebuild. Files File Responsibility FullMeshNetworkBuilder.cs Connects every pair of mutually visible devices, producing a full-visibility mesh. Edges grow as O(n^2). This mirrors the original implicit behaviour of the engine. Use a limited-degree or spanning-tree builder for larger networks. Extension points Add new builders by implementing INetworkBuilder from Engine/Routers/Contracts/. Set SimulationEngine.Instance.NetworkBuilder at runtime to switch strategies without restarting. Call SimulationEngine.Instance.RebuildTopology() to apply the new builder to the current device set. Parent See Engine/Routers/Documentation.md for the full routing subsystem description.

Routers/Contracts/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Routers/Contracts | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Routers/Contracts/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Routers/Contracts This folder holds all interfaces that define the routing and topology contracts. Nothing in this folder has an implementation; implementations live in peer folders. Files File Responsibility IPacketRouter.cs Contract for a packet routing strategy. Exposes Name and Route(packet, sender, topology). The active instance is stored on SimulationEngine.Router. INetworkBuilder.cs Contract for a network-formation strategy. Exposes Name and Build(devices, topology). Called by SimulationEngine whenever the device registry changes. INetworkTopology.cs Read-only topology view: GetVisibleDevices, GetConnectedDevices, AreVisible, AreConnected. Passed to routers and exposed as SimulationEngine.Topology. IMutableNetworkTopology.cs Extends INetworkTopology with Connect, Disconnect, RemoveDevice, ClearConnections. Passed only to builders; never exposed to routers or UI. Extension points Implement IPacketRouter to add a new routing algorithm. Implement INetworkBuilder to add a new mesh-formation strategy. Both can be set on SimulationEngine at runtime without restarting the simulation. Parent See Engine/Routers/Documentation.md for the full routing subsystem description.

Routers/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Routers | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Routers/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Routers The Routers folder contains the complete routing and network-topology subsystem. It is organized into subfolders by responsibility so that contracts, topology, builders, and each router strategy are cleanly separated. Subfolders Subfolder Responsibility Contracts/ Interfaces that define the routing and topology contracts: IPacketRouter, INetworkBuilder, INetworkTopology, IMutableNetworkTopology. Topology/ The concrete NetworkTopology implementation of IMutableNetworkTopology. Builders/ Network-formation strategy implementations. Currently contains FullMeshNetworkBuilder. Flooding/ The FloodingPacketRouter - naive broadcast to all visible neighbours. Engine default. SmartFlooding/ The SmartFloodingPacketRouter - direct delivery if destination visible, filtered flood otherwise. WebApp default. Design principles Visibility vs. Connection - visibility is a Euclidean-distance relation computed on-the-fly; connection is an explicit link established by the active INetworkBuilder. Flooding uses visibility; unicast routing uses connections. Hot-swap - both SimulationEngine.Router and SimulationEngine.NetworkBuilder can be replaced at runtime to compare strategies without restarting the simulation. Separation of mutation - routers receive INetworkTopology (read-only); only builders receive IMutableNetworkTopology. Namespace All files in this tree use the namespace Engine.Routers regardless of subfolder, preserving backward compatibility with consumers that already using Engine.Routers;. Parent See Engine/Documentation.md for the full engine architecture.

Routers/Flooding/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Routers/Flooding | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Routers/Flooding/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Routers/Flooding This folder contains the FloodingPacketRouter, the engine's default routing strategy. Files File Responsibility PacketRouter.cs FloodingPacketRouter - implements IPacketRouter. On each Route call it broadcasts a shallow clone of the packet to every device that is visible (not just connected) to the sender. Uses visibility to ensure delivery even before a connection graph is established. Strategy characteristics Reliability - delivers to all reachable neighbours regardless of connection state. Overhead - produces O(visible-neighbours) clones per hop, growing exponentially in dense grids without TTL capping. High duplicate-delivery rate. Use case - engine default; useful as a baseline for benchmark comparisons. Parent See Engine/Routers/Documentation.md for the full routing subsystem description.

Routers/SmartFlooding/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Routers/SmartFlooding | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Routers/SmartFlooding/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Routers/SmartFlooding This folder contains the SmartFloodingPacketRouter, the WebApp's default routing strategy and the improved alternative to FloodingPacketRouter. Files File Responsibility SmartFloodingPacketRouter.cs SmartFloodingPacketRouter - implements IPacketRouter. Applies three optimisations over naive flooding: (1) direct delivery if the destination is a visible neighbour, skipping the full broadcast; (2) no reverse-path forward back to PreviousHop; (3) no echo back to the original From device. Strategy characteristics Reliability - guaranteed delivery in any connected topology with sufficient TTL. Overhead - substantially fewer clones than naive flooding; in sparse topologies may reach near-unicast efficiency. Use case - WebApp default (SimulationConfig.SelectedRouter); set by SimulationService on construction. Parent See Engine/Routers/Documentation.md for the full routing subsystem description.

Routers/Topology/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Routers/Topology | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Routers/Topology/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Routers/Topology This folder contains the concrete implementation of the network topology used by the engine at runtime. Files File Responsibility NetworkTopology.cs Implements IMutableNetworkTopology. Maintains an in-memory adjacency set of connections (bidirectional, GUIDs). Visibility is computed on-the-fly from device positions and SimulationEngine.VisibilityDistance - it is never cached. Owned exclusively by SimulationEngine; exposed as INetworkTopology through SimulationEngine.Topology. Key behaviors Visibility queries are O(n) over all registered devices. Connection queries are O(1) via hash-set lookup. ClearConnections is called by every INetworkBuilder.Build invocation before rewriting the graph. RemoveDevice cleans up all edges incident to the removed device to prevent stale references. Parent See Engine/Routers/Documentation.md for the full routing subsystem description.

Statistics/Documentation.html.title: Engine/Statistics | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
Statistics/Documentation.html.summary: Engine/Statistics The Statistics folder contains the reactive simulation metrics subsystem. It listens to SimulationEngine events and accumulates observable metric values and a per-tick history ring buffer. Files File Responsibility SimulationStatistics.cs Singleton that subscribes to all SimulationEngine events and maintains ten StatMetric values. Appends a TickSnapshot to the history ring buffer on every tick. Raises Updated after each change so UI can re-render reactively. StatMetric.cs A single named metric with a double Value, display format flags (isDecimal, isPlottable), and an Updated event. Increment() and Set(value) update the value and notify subscribers. TickSnapshot.cs Immutable record capturing a point-in-time snapshot of the eight plottable metrics for one tick. Used for time-series chart rendering. Metrics tracked Metric Description Packets registered All enqueued packets including flood clones. Packets delivered Packets that reached their destination. Packets expired Packets dropped because TTL hit zero. Devices added Cumulative device registrations since last reset. Total ticks Engine ticks elapsed since last reset. Avg tick (ms) Running mean of wall-clock time per tick. Active packets Packets currently in-flight. Delivery rate (%) Delivered / (Delivered + Expired) x 100. Duplicate deliveries Flood clones that arrived after first delivery of the same logical packet. Avg hop count Mean InitialTtl - TTL at first delivery. Extension points Add a new StatMetric field to SimulationStatistics, subscribe to the relevant engine event, and set isPlottable: true to expose it as a chart series. Parent See Engine/Documentation.md for the full engine architecture.

api/Engine.Benchmark.BenchmarkConfig.htm.title: Class BenchmarkConfig | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
api/Engine.Benchmark.BenchmarkConfig.htm.summary: Class BenchmarkConfig Namespace Engine.Benchmark Assembly Engine.dll Fully serialisable description of a benchmark scenario. A BenchmarkConfig defines everything needed to reproduce a headless simulation run deterministically: Simulation engine settings (visibility, TTL, packet travel time). The initial network layout as a list of DeviceBenchmarkDto. A timeline of BenchmarkEventEntry items that modify the network at specific ticks (toggle, add, remove device). The total number of ticks to simulate. The list of router names to test; BenchmarkRunner will run one full simulation per router and collect results for comparison. Instances are stored as JSON together with their BenchmarkSession results so a test can be replayed or shared. public sealed class BenchmarkConfig Inheritance object BenchmarkConfig Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties DefaultTtl Default TTL assigned to new packets. public int DefaultTtl { get; set; } Property Value int Description Optional free-text description. public string Description { get; set; } Property Value string Devices Devices present at the start of the simulation. Applied in declaration order via RegisterDevice(Device). public List<DeviceBenchmarkDto> Devices { get; set; } Property Value List<DeviceBenchmarkDto> DurationTicks Total number of engine ticks to run per router. public long DurationTicks { get; set; } Property Value long Events Ordered list of events to apply at specific ticks. Sorted by AtTick before the run starts. public List<BenchmarkEventEntry> Events { get; set; } Property Value List<BenchmarkEventEntry> MaxActivePackets Maximum simultaneously in-flight packets (0 = auto: devices ? 2000). public int MaxActivePackets { get; set; } Property Value int Name User-assigned name shown in the UI list. public string Name { get; set; } Property Value string RouterNames Names of routers to test, matched against Name. The runner iterates this list and executes one full simulation per entry. public List<string> RouterNames { get; set; } Property Value List<string> TicksToTravel Ticks a packet spends in transit between adjacent nodes. public int TicksToTravel { get; set; } Property Value int VisibilityDistance Visibility distance applied to the engine during the run. public int VisibilityDistance { get; set; } Property Value int

api/Engine.Benchmark.BenchmarkRunner.htm.title: Class BenchmarkRunner | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
api/Engine.Benchmark.BenchmarkRunner.htm.summary: Class BenchmarkRunner Namespace Engine.Benchmark Assembly Engine.dll Executes a BenchmarkConfig headlessly - without any UI - and produces a BenchmarkSession containing one BenchmarkResult per configured router. Threading: designed to be called from Task.Run so that the Blazor UI thread stays responsive during the (potentially long) run. All engine interaction happens through the singleton Instance which is fully reset between runs. Progress reporting: the optional onProgress callback in Run(BenchmarkConfig, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, IPacketRouter>, Action<int, int>?) is invoked after each completed router run with (routerIndex, totalRouters) so callers can display a spinner. public static class BenchmarkRunner Inheritance object BenchmarkRunner Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Run(BenchmarkConfig, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, IPacketRouter>, Action<int, int>?) Runs the benchmark described by config synchronously. Call this inside Task.Run() to avoid blocking the UI thread. public static BenchmarkSession Run(BenchmarkConfig config, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, IPacketRouter> availableRouters, Action<int, int>? onProgress = null) Parameters config BenchmarkConfig The benchmark configuration to execute. availableRouters IReadOnlyDictionary<string, IPacketRouter> All known IPacketRouter implementations, keyed by Name. Routers listed in RouterNames that are not found here are silently skipped. onProgress Action<int, int> Optional callback invoked after each router completes: (completedCount, totalCount). Called on the thread-pool thread, not the Blazor synchronisation context. Returns BenchmarkSession A BenchmarkSession with results for every router that was found and executed.

api/Engine.Core.SimulationEngine.html.title: Class SimulationEngine | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
api/Engine.Core.SimulationEngine.html.summary: Class SimulationEngine Namespace Engine.Core Assembly Engine.dll The central singleton that drives the entire simulation. It maintains the device registry, owns the tick-based time model, and manages the priority queue of in-flight packets. Every call to Tick() advances the simulation by one step: the tick counter is incremented, wall-clock delta time is measured, all subscribers to TickEvent are notified, and any packets whose ArrivalTick has been reached are dequeued and dispatched to their NextHop device. Routing strategy and network-formation strategy are fully replaceable at runtime via Router and NetworkBuilder. The read-only topology view is exposed through Topology. public class SimulationEngine Inheritance object SimulationEngine Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE The multiplier used to compute the automatic packet limit when MaxActivePackets is set to 0. Default: 2000 packets per registered device. public const int AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE = 2000 Field Value int VISIBILITY_DISTANCE The default visibility distance used when none is configured explicitly. public const int VISIBILITY_DISTANCE = 200 Field Value int Properties ActivePackets Gets a read-only snapshot of all packets currently in-flight. public IReadOnlyList<Packet> ActivePackets { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<Packet> Remarks This allocates a new List<T> on every call. Use ActivePacketsCount when only the count is needed. ActivePacketsCount Gets the number of packets currently in-flight without allocating a list. Prefer this over ActivePackets.Count in hot paths such as statistics collection. public int ActivePacketsCount { get; } Property Value int Devices Gets a read-only snapshot of all devices registered in the simulation. public IReadOnlyList<Device> Devices { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<Device> EffectivePacketLimit Returns the effective packet limit that will be enforced on the next RegisterPacket(Packet) call. If MaxActivePackets > 0 - returns that value. Otherwise - returns Devices.Count x AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE. Returns MaxValue when there are no devices yet so that early registration during setup never triggers the guard. public int EffectivePacketLimit { get; } Property Value int Hub Gets the hub device registered in this simulation, or null if no HubDevice has been added yet. public Device? Hub { get; } Property Value Device Instance Gets the singleton instance of SimulationEngine. public static SimulationEngine Instance { get; } Property Value SimulationEngine MaxActivePackets Gets or sets the maximum number of packets that may be simultaneously in-flight in the simulation. When set to 0 (the default) the limit is computed automatically as Devices.Count x AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE each time a packet is enqueued, so it scales with the network size. When the in-flight count reaches or exceeds the effective limit, RegisterPacket(Packet) throws PacketLimitExceededException and the tick loop must be stopped by the caller. Set to a positive value to override the automatic limit. public int MaxActivePackets { get; set; } Property Value int NetworkBuilder Gets or sets the active network-formation strategy. Defaults to FullMeshNetworkBuilder. Changing this property does not automatically rebuild the topology; call RebuildTopology() to apply the new builder to the current device set. public INetworkBuilder NetworkBuilder { get; set; } Property Value INetworkBuilder Router Gets or sets the active packet-routing strategy. Defaults to FloodingPacketRouter. Can be swapped at runtime (e.g. from the UI) to compare different routing protocols on the same network without restarting the simulation. public IPacketRouter Router { get; set; } Property Value IPacketRouter TickCount Gets the total number of ticks that have elapsed since the simulation started. public long TickCount { get; } Property Value long Topology Gets the read-only view of the current network topology (visibility and established connections). Always up-to-date: visibility is computed on-the-fly, connections are rebuilt by NetworkBuilder on every device-registry change. public INetworkTopology Topology { get; } Property Value INetworkTopology VisibilityDistance The effective visibility distance for this simulation instance. Defaults to VISIBILITY_DISTANCE; can be changed at runtime. Changing this value does not automatically rebuild the topology - call RebuildTopology() explicitly if needed. public int VisibilityDistance { get; set; } Property Value int Methods RebuildTopology() Triggers the active NetworkBuilder to recompute the logical connection graph for the current device set. Called automatically on every device-registry change (RegisterDevice(Device), RemoveDevice(Guid)). Can also be called manually - for example after moving a device or after replacing NetworkBuilder - to immediately reflect the new topology without waiting for the next device change. public void RebuildTopology() RegisterDevice(Device) Adds a device to the simulation. If the device is a HubDevice it is also stored in Hub. The topology is rebuilt via RebuildTopology() after the device is added. public void RegisterDevice(Device device) Parameters device Device RegisterPacket(Packet) Enqueues a packet into the in-flight priority queue. The absolute arrival tick is computed as TickCount + packet.TicksToTravel and stored on the packet. public void RegisterPacket(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet Exceptions PacketLimitExceededException Thrown when the number of in-flight packets is already at or above EffectivePacketLimit before the new packet is added. The caller (typically the simulation tick loop) must stop the simulation and surface this error to the user. RemoveDevice(Guid) Removes a previously registered device by its Id. The topology is updated (device connections removed) and then rebuilt. public void RemoveDevice(Guid id) Parameters id Guid Reset() Removes all registered devices and discards all in-flight packets, resetting the engine to its initial empty state. public void Reset() Tick() Advances the simulation by one tick. Increments TickCount. Measures wall-clock delta time since the last tick. Fires TickEvent so that subscribed devices can act. Dispatches all packets that are due to arrive this tick. Raises Ticked. public (long tick, double dt) Tick() Returns (long tick, double dt) Events DeviceRegistered Raised after a new device is added to the simulation. public event EventHandler<DeviceRegisteredEventArgs>? PacketDelivered Event Type EventHandler<PacketDeliveredEventArgs> PacketExpired Raised when a packet's TTL reaches zero and it is silently dropped. public event EventHandler<PacketExpiredEventArgs>? PacketExpired Event Type EventHandler<PacketExpiredEventArgs> PacketRegistered Raised every time a packet is enqueued (including flood clones). public event EventHandler<PacketRegisteredEventArgs>? api/Engine.Devices.Device.html.title: Class Device | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
api/Engine.Devices.Device.html.summary: Class Device Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll Abstract base class for all devices in the simulation network. A device has a unique identity and a position in 2D space. When it receives a packet it either forwards it (if it is not the intended recipient) or accepts and processes it. Forwarding is delegated to RoutePacket(Packet, Device) so that the active IPacketRouter is always consulted. Devices never reference a concrete router directly. public abstract class Device Inheritance object Device Derived EmitterDevice GeneratorDevice HubDevice Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Id Gets the unique identifier of this device. public Guid Id { get; } Property Value Guid Name Gets or sets the human-readable name of this device. public string Name { get; set; } Property Value string Position Gets or sets the 2D position of this device in the simulation world. public Vector2 Position { get; set; } Property Value Vector2 Methods Accept(Packet) Processes a packet that is addressed to and has been delivered to this device. Derived classes implement the specific handling logic here. public abstract void Accept(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The delivered packet to process. Recieve(Packet) Called when a packet arrives at this device. If the packet is addressed to another device, routing is delegated to RoutePacket(Packet, Device) which uses the currently configured IPacketRouter. If the packet is addressed to this device and delivery confirmation was requested, a ConfirmationPacket is routed back to the originator before Accept(Packet) is invoked. public void Recieve(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The packet that has arrived at this device.

api/Engine.Devices.EmitterDevice.html.title: Class EmitterDevice | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
api/Engine.Devices.EmitterDevice.html.summary: Class EmitterDevice Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll A device that receives command packets from the hub and applies a boolean state to itself (e.g. turning an actuator on or off). When ControlFrequencyTicks is greater than zero the hub automatically sends a ControlPacket to this device every ControlFrequencyTicks ticks, toggling its state from on to off and back. Set ControlFrequencyTicks to 0 to disable automatic control signals. Flooding note: with FloodingPacketRouter a unicast ControlPacket is re-broadcast at every intermediate hop, creating an exponential number of clones (approximately neighbours^TTL in the worst case). The TTL is therefore capped at CONTROL_PACKET_TTL rather than the default packet TTL. When a unicast router is active this has no extra cost because the packet follows a single path. public class EmitterDevice : Device Inheritance object Device EmitterDevice Inherited Members Device.Name Device.Id Device.Position Device.Recieve(Packet) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors EmitterDevice(long) Initialises the emitter and subscribes to the engine tick event so that automatic hub-to-device control signals can be sent. public EmitterDevice(long controlFrequencyTicks = 0) Parameters controlFrequencyTicks long Interval in ticks between automatic control packets from the hub. Pass 0 to start with automatic control disabled. Fields CONTROL_PACKET_TTL TTL assigned to outgoing ControlPacket instances. Lower than the default packet TTL to limit flooding clone counts. api/Engine.Devices.html.summary: Namespace Engine.Devices Classes Device Abstract base class for all devices in the simulation network. A device has a unique identity and a position in 2D space. When it receives a packet it either forwards it (if it is not the intended recipient) or accepts and processes it. Forwarding is delegated to RoutePacket(Packet, Device) so that the active IPacketRouter is always consulted. Devices never reference a concrete router directly. EmitterDevice A device that receives command packets from the hub and applies a boolean state to itself (e.g. turning an actuator on or off). When ControlFrequencyTicks is greater than zero the hub automatically sends a ControlPacket to this device every ControlFrequencyTicks ticks, toggling its state from on to off and back. Set ControlFrequencyTicks to 0 to disable automatic control signals. Flooding note: with FloodingPacketRouter a unicast ControlPacket is re-broadcast at every intermediate hop, creating an exponential number of clones (approximately neighbours^TTL in the worst case). The TTL is therefore capped at CONTROL_PACKET_TTL rather than the default packet TTL. When a unicast router is active this has no extra cost because the packet follows a single path. api/Engine.Routers.FloodingPacketRouter..summary: Class FloodingPacketRouter Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Implements a flooding broadcast routing strategy. When asked to route a packet the router queries the supplied INetworkTopology for all devices visible to the sender and enqueues a cloned copy addressed to each of them. Flooding uses visibility - not established connections - because it is inherently a broadcast mechanism that needs no prior topology knowledge. This class is no longer a singleton. The active router instance is stored on Router and is injected into every routing call automatically by Recieve(Packet). To change the routing strategy at runtime replace Router with a different IPacketRouter implementation. public class FloodingPacketRouter : IPacketRouter Inheritance object FloodingPacketRouter Implements IPacketRouter Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Name Gets the human-readable name of this routing strategy. Shown in the UI when the user selects a routing protocol. public string Name { get; } Property Value string Methods Route(Packet, Device, INetworkTopology) Routes packet from sender. The implementation decides which device(s) receive clones of the packet on the next hop. public void Route(Packet packet, Device sender, INetworkTopology topology) Parameters packet Packet The packet to route. sender Device The device currently holding the packet. topology INetworkTopology The current network topology. Used to discover neighbours or established connections reachable from sender. Remarks Broadcasts packet to every device that is within VisibilityDistance of sender. Each visible neighbour receives its own shallow clone so that per-hop fields (NextHop, ArrivalTick) can be set independently.

api/Engine.Routers.FullMeshNetworkBuilde.summary: Class FullMeshNetworkBuilder Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll A network-formation strategy that connects every pair of mutually visible devices, producing a full-visibility mesh. This mirrors the original implicit behaviour of the engine where visibility and connectivity were equivalent: if two devices can see each other they are considered connected. Note: as the number of devices grows, the number of edges grows as O(n?). For large networks consider a limited-degree or spanning-tree builder instead. public sealed class FullMeshNetworkBuilder : INetworkBuilder Inheritance object FullMeshNetworkBuilder Implements INetworkBuilder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Name Gets the human-readable name of this network-formation strategy. Shown in the UI when the user selects a mesh protocol. public string Name { get; } Property Value string Methods Build(IReadOnlyList<Device>, IMutableNetworkTopology) Rebuilds the logical topology for the given set of devices. Called by SimulationEngine whenever the device registry changes (device added / removed) or when an explicit rebuild is requested (e.g. on topology-change events in future dynamic-mesh protocols). public void Build(IReadOnlyList<Device> devices, IMutableNetworkTopology topology) Parameters devices IReadOnlyList<Device> The current full device list from Devices. The builder must treat this as a read-only snapshot. topology IMutableNetworkTopology The topology object to update. The builder calls its mutating methods to add or remove connections; it must not replace the instance itself because routers and statistics may hold a reference to it. Remarks Clears all existing connections and then connects every pair (i, j) where i < j and the two devices are mutually visible, using AreVisible(Device, Device).

api/Engine.Routers.INetworkBuilder.html.title: Interface INetworkBuilder | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
api/Engine.Routers.INetworkBuilder.html.summary: Interface INetworkBuilder Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Defines the contract for a network-formation (mesh-building) strategy. A network builder is responsible for deciding which devices establish explicit connections with each other, forming the logical mesh topology. It operates on top of raw visibility data and produces an INetworkTopology that the active IPacketRouter then uses for forwarding decisions. Examples of algorithms that implement this interface: Full-mesh - every visible pair is connected (current implicit behaviour). Limited-degree mesh - each device connects to at most K neighbours. Minimum spanning tree - minimises total edge weight. Cluster-head / LEACH-style hierarchy. Like IPacketRouter, the active builder is stored on SimulationEngine and can be swapped at runtime from the UI to compare how different formation strategies affect routing statistics. public interface INetworkBuilder Properties Name Gets the human-readable name of this network-formation strategy. Shown in the UI when the user selects a mesh protocol. string Name { get; } Property Value string Methods Build(IReadOnlyList<Device>, IMutableNetworkTopology) Rebuilds the logical topology for the given set of devices. Called by SimulationEngine whenever the device registry changes (device added / removed) or when an explicit rebuild is requested (e.g. on topology-change events in future dynamic-mesh protocols). void Build(IReadOnlyList<Device> devices, IMutableNetworkTopology topology) Parameters devices IReadOnlyList<Device> The current full device list from Devices. The builder must treat this as a read-only snapshot. topology IMutableNetworkTopology The topology object to update. api/Engine.Routers.INetworkTopology.html.title: Interface INetworkTopology | IoT Swarm Mesh - Simulation Engine
api/Engine.Routers.INetworkTopology.html.summary: Interface INetworkTopology Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Represents the logical topology of the network at a given point in time. The topology separates two concepts that are intentionally kept distinct: Visibility Whether two devices are within radio range of each other. Used for broadcast/discovery and as a precondition for establishing a connection. Determined purely by Euclidean distance and VisibilityDistance. Connection An explicit, mutually agreed logical link between two devices. A connection can only exist between mutually visible devices, but not every visible device is necessarily connected (subject to per-device connection limits, protocol decisions, etc.). Unicast routing and network-topology algorithms operate on connections, not on raw visibility. Implementations are owned and updated by the active INetworkBuilder and are passed to IPacketRouter on every routing call so that routers never need to touch SimulationEngine directly. public interface INetworkTopology Methods AreConnected(Device, Device) Returns true when a and b have an established logical connection. bool AreConnected(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device Returns bool AreVisible(Device, Device) Returns true when a and b are within radio range of each other. bool AreVisible(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device Returns bool GetConnectedDevices(Device) Returns the devices that are logically connected to device. index.html.summary: IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Documentation Welcome to the IoT Swarm Mesh simulation engine documentation. This site is generated from source XML comments and hand-written conceptual guides using DocFX. The Engine library is the self-contained simulation core of the IoT Swarm Mesh project. It models a wireless IoT network on a 2D plane: devices are placed at arbitrary positions, data packets flow between them tick-by-tick, and a pluggable routing algorithm propagates data toward the central hub. No UI dependencies - any host that calls SimulationEngine.Instance.Tick() can drive the simulation (console, Blazor WASM, test harness).
